# UC-18.3.4 · VXLAN Tunnel and Overlay Health: VTEP Reachability, BGP-EVPN Convergence, and Underlay Path-MTU Hygiene

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Availability, Fault, Performance, Inventory, Configuration &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch whether the invisible tunnel endpoints between switches stay healthy and whether the fabric keeps publishing overlay reachability for MAC and IP addresses. When physical paths shrink packets too small for VXLAN wrappers—or routes churn during workload moves—we raise it early before east-west traffic fails quietly.*

---

## Description

Observes vendor-neutral **VXLAN** overlay dataplane posture across **BGP-EVPN** fabrics—tracking **VTEP** neighbor availability (**NVE** peers evaporating, **`underlay`** **`UDP`** **`4789`** transport faults), **AFI 25 / SAFI 70** route churn (**Type 2 MAC/IP**, **Type 3 inclusive multicast**, **Type 5 IP-prefix**, **Type 4 Ethernet-Segment / DF**, **Type 1 auto-discovery**) mirrored into Splunk fields, **VNI-to-bridge-domain** integrity (**silent black-hole** cues when **`MAC`** **`mobility`** storms erupt), **underlay path-MTU** shrink observations (**ICMP PMTU**, **`fragment`** **`drops`** narratives beneath **1554-byte** recommended floors tied to **50-byte** **`VXLAN`** encapsulation overhead vs **1500-byte** workload frames), **route-target import/export drift** fingerprints (**VRF leak** wording inside syslog), **multi-site Type-5 prefix** oscillations (**mobility / stretched subnets**), **Ethernet Segment ID** **`designated-forwarder`** instability (**duplicate DF**, **`single-active`**/`all-active` imbalance hints), **BUM replication / ingress replication** counters (**head-end replication**, **`vxlan flood`** warnings), and leaf-level drop counters correlated against **`peer_state`** timelines—distinct from generic northbound **`SDN`** **`controller`** **`cluster`** **`membership`** telemetry catalogued separately inside sibling **`UC`** entries.

## Value

When **VTEP** adjacency flickers yet **`underlay`** optics stay green, east-west latency hides inside **`EVPN`** **`NLRI`** churn long before **`ping`** drills fail—merging **`NVE`** **`peer`** timelines, **`BGP-EVPN`** **`route-type`** rollups, **`PMTU`** **`evidence`**, **`ESI`** **`DF`** narratives, **`BUM`** replication counters, and multi-vendor syslog **SC4S** fan-in collapses redundant war rooms between **`NetOps`**, virtualization SREs, and transport engineers who otherwise debate whether the fault sits in **`leaf`**, **`spine`**, **`RR`**, or guest migrations **vMotion** kicked off.

## Implementation

Enable **Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus**, **Splunk_TA_arista**, **Splunk_TA_junos** syslog plus optional **JTI** **`openconfig`** streams; schedule collectors landing **`vxlan:*`** sourcetypes; normalize **`FIELDALIAS`** for **`vni`**, **`peer_vtep`**, **`evpn_route_type`**, **`underlay_mtu`**, **`leaf_hostname`**; route **`overlay_fault`** alerts through CMDB **`fabric_site`** ownership; suppress maintenance using automation **`CHG`** tokens plus hypervisor **`maintenance`** flags.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Splunk TA footprint — Deploy Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus (Splunkbase 6876), Splunk_TA_arista (6797), and Splunk_TA_junos (2847) where NX-OS, EOS, and Junos syslog lands; preserve syslog PRI fidelity via Splunk Connect for Syslog when fanning many leaf switches through regional relays.
- Telemetry lane — Subscribe Junos Telemetry Interface or gNMI streams into UDP receivers that emit juniper:junos:jti:openconfig JSON mapped from OpenConfig paths network-instance/vxlan/vni and network-instance/protocols/bgp/neighbors into Splunk fields vni, peer_vtep, underlay_mtu, neighbor_state.
- Scripted snapshots — Schedule Ansible, Nornir, SaltStack, or Python pollers issuing NX-OS **show nve peers**, EOS **show vxlan vtep**, Junos **show route table bgp.evpn.0**, FRR **show bgp l2vpn evpn**, plus ICMP-style **PMTU** probes feeding vxlan:vtep:reachability, vxlan:evpn:routes, vxlan:underlay:mtu, vxlan:bum:replication with leaf_hostname, fabric_site, host keys stamped every poll.
- Protocol anchors — Encode knowledge that **VXLAN** uses **UDP** destination **4789** (IANA), optional legacy **8472** on certain Linux stacks, **RFC 7348** framing, roughly **50-byte** outer overhead atop tenant Ethernet frames, and **BGP-EVPN** **AFI 25 / SAFI 70** carrying **Type-1**/**Type-2**/**Type-3**/**Type-4**/**Type-5** NLRI per **RFC 7432**/**RFC 8365**—these anchors justify Splunk thresholds referencing **1554-byte** minimum underlay MTU when preserving **1500-byte** workloads.
- Credentials — Separate collector identities per vendor API (HTTPS eAPI, NETCONF over SSH, gNMI TLS); never embed personal operator passwords inside modular inputs audited quarterly.

### Step 1 — Land vendor evidence
(1) **Cisco Nexus 9000** — Ingest **cisco:nxos:syslog**; add optional REST toward **dme/sys/eps/epId-*/peers** or nightly **show nve peers** dumps serialized into vxlan:vtep:reachability so Splunk correlates **NVE** peer tables with syslog cues (**nve**, **peer**, **down** strings).
(2) **Arista EOS** — Normalize **arista:eos:syslog** and CloudVision-fed telemetry into identical vxlan:* schemas via eAPI JSON-RPC (**show vxlan vtep**, **show bgp evpn**) so leaf_hostname stays aligned between telemetry and syslog storms.
(3) **Juniper QFX** — Combine **juniper:junos:syslog** with **juniper:junos:jti:openconfig**; when Apstra publishes intent drift events separately, optionally join via lookup tables keyed by device serial—not blended blindly into vxlan sourcetypes unless fields match.
(4) **Linux / SONiC / Cumulus** — Forward **vtysh** JSON or Prometheus scrapes converted through HEC into vxlan:evpn:routes so software **VTEPs** participate without bespoke SPL forks.
(5) **HEC hygiene** — Issue distinct HEC tokens per automation pipeline (Ansible Tower, Salt Syndic) so blast-radius isolation stays intact when credentials rotate.

### Step 2 — Normalize fields and publish the saved search
(1) Build FIELDALIAS transforms translating vendor-specific keys (**nve-peer-ip**, **remote-vtep**, **esi**, **df-state**, **path-mtu**) into canonical peer_vtep, evpn_route_type, esi_id, designated_forwarder, underlay_mtu columns used in macros.
(2) Install the UC **spl** body as saved search vxlan_overlay_health_core with five- to fifteen-minute cadence; keep earliest=-4h@m inside the saved search or override via macro token for investigations.
(3) Wrap maintenance correlation macros referencing CMDB fabric_site tags plus approved change identifiers so overlay_fault storms during flagged windows downgrade paging severity automatically when policy allows.
(4) Capture SPL revisions inside Git with reviewer notes anytime eval clauses shift—for example when introducing Arista-specific regex aliases alongside Cisco shorthand.
(5) Budget indexer load: vxlan:* JSON snapshots may spike EPS during polling bursts—coalesce redundant polls per leaf per minute before writing HEC payloads.

```spl
(index=network_ops OR index=dc_fabric OR index=sdn_ops OR index=os OR index=cisco_dc)
(sourcetype="vxlan:vtep:reachability" OR sourcetype="vxlan:evpn:routes" OR sourcetype="vxlan:underlay:mtu" OR sourcetype="vxlan:bum:replication" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:syslog" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:syslog" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:jti:openconfig")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval route_family=upper(trim(coalesce(evpn_route_type, nlri_rt, bgp_evpn_rt, route_type, "")))
| eval rt_bucket=case(
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?2|MAC.?IP"), "RT2_MACIP_TYPE2",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?3|IMET|INCLUSIVE"), "RT3_IMET_TYPE3",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?5|PREFIX"), "RT5_IP_PREFIX_TYPE5",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?4|ESI|DF"), "RT4_ESI_DF_TYPE4",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?1|AUTO.?DISCOVERY"), "RT1_AD_TYPE1",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)mac.?mobility"), "RT2_MACIP_TYPE2",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)type.?5"), "RT5_IP_PREFIX_TYPE5",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)inclusive|imet"), "RT3_IMET_TYPE3",
    "RT_UNSPECIFIED")
| eval vxlan_udp_port=tonumber(coalesce(dst_udp_port, udp_dport, udp_dest_port, vxlan_udp_port, 4789))
| eval outer_mtu=tonumber(coalesce(underlay_mtu, pmtu, path_mtu, ""))
| eval vni_key=tonumber(coalesce(vni, vxlan_vni, evi_id, l2_vni, ""))
| eval dst_vtep=trim(coalesce(vtep_peer_ip, peer_vtep, nve_peer, dst_vtep_ip, ""))
| eval peer_state=lower(trim(coalesce(nve_peer_state, peer_oper_state, vtep_peer_state, reach_state, "")))
| eval overlay_fault=case(
    peer_state IN ("down","gone","inactive","failed","missing") OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(nve.*down|peer.*lost|vtep.*down|tunnel.*down)"), "loss_vtep_reachability",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(bgp.*reset|evpn.*flap|notification.*sent|afi.?25.*sa.?70|l2vpn.?evpn)"), "evpn_control_plane_flap",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(mac.?move|mobility|duplicate.?mac|sticky)"), "type2_mac_mobility_storm",
    isnotnull(outer_mtu) AND outer_mtu>0 AND outer_mtu<1554, "underlay_mtu_below_vxlan_budget",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(esi.*duplicate|df.*election|ether.?segment|single.?active|all.?active)"), "esi_df_instability",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(bum|ingress.?repl|vxlan.?flood|unknown.?unicast)"), "bum_replication_anomaly",
    null())
| where isnotnull(overlay_fault)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
    latest(peer_state) as peer_oper_latest,
    latest(rt_bucket) as evpn_route_class,
    latest(vxlan_udp_port) as udp_port_seen,
    latest(outer_mtu) as mtu_observed,
    dc(vni_key) as vn_segment_count,
    values(dst_vtep) as peer_list,
    count as hits
  by overlay_fault, leaf_hostname, fabric_site, host
| eval last_seen_ts=strftime(last_seen,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| sort -last_seen
| head 350
| table overlay_fault leaf_hostname fabric_site peer_oper_latest evpn_route_class udp_port_seen mtu_observed vn_segment_count peer_list hits last_seen_ts last_seen
```

Correlation notes — **rt_bucket** lumps NLRI hints into RT2_MACIP_TYPE2, RT3_IMET_TYPE3, RT5_IP_PREFIX_TYPE5, RT4_ESI_DF_TYPE4, RT1_AD_TYPE1 buckets even when syslog lacks structured typing—regex hits **mac mobility**, **imet**, **prefix**, **esi**, **df** tokens inside **_raw**. **vxlan_udp_port** surfaces accidental **8472** deployments touching hardware peers expecting **4789**. **outer_mtu** comparisons highlight PMTU shrink paths under **1554** bytes—tune numeric cut if your campus only ever provisions **9000-byte** jumbos end-to-end. **overlay_fault** ordering lists **loss_vtep_reachability** before **type2_mac_mobility_storm** to keep neighbor-down evidence ahead of benign MAC move noise. **vn_segment_count** uses **dc(vni_key)** strictly as diversity signal—pair with manual **show vxlan vni** if counts look absurd due to parsing bugs.

### Step 3 — Validate against live CLI/API snapshots
(1) Choose Splunk rows flagged **loss_vtep_reachability** — SSH to the leaf and compare **show nve peers** Peer-state versus peer_oper_latest; drift beyond two polling cycles signals stale collectors or duplicate syslog hostnames behind NAT.
(2) On EOS compare **show vxlan vtep** Remote IP addresses against peer_list values—UDP counters should increment on port **4789** unless policy maps alternate UDP sockets (validate **udp_port_seen**).
(3) On Junos run **show route table bgp.evpn.0 detail** examining Ethernet Segment routes (**Type 4**) whenever esi_df_instability fires—confirm DF winner matches designated_forwarder telemetry when streamed.
(4) For **underlay_mtu_below_vxlan_budget**, inspect physical uplinks **show interface counters errors** hunting fragment drops or giants unrelated to overlay syslog wording yet indicative of WAN policing squeezing MTU upstream of the leaf.
(5) Cross-verify fabric_site tagging inside Splunk lookups against Apstra blueprint or NDFC site names—mis-tags break suppression macros tied to maintenance events.

### Step 4 — Operationalise dashboards and alert routing
(1) Dashboard Studio or Simple XML workspace: tiles for overlay_fault mixes, sparkline hits by fabric_site, heatmap evpn_route_class × leaf_hostname, drilldown tables showing peer_list, mtu_observed, udp_port_seen—annotate with approved change overlays when possible.
(2) Route **loss_vtep_reachability** and **esi_df_instability** to fabric routing owners; route **underlay_mtu_below_vxlan_budget** jointly with WAN transport partners; route **type2_mac_mobility_storm** plus virtualization liaison contacts when suppression lookups show hypervisor migration waves.
(3) Weekly CSV snapshots archived for auditors proving UDP **4789** dataplane telemetry sits alongside Inventory-oriented EVPN snapshots referenced by hybrid Network_Traffic + Inventory posture reviews.
(4) Tune throttle thresholds per overlay_fault class—BGP churn tolerances differ between spine RR failures versus leaf **NVE** faults.
(5) Document escalation ladders distinguishing multicast-core instability (**bum_replication_anomaly**) from RR-specific BGP resets (**evpn_control_plane_flap**) so responders avoid swapping optics when Rendezvous Point maintenance explains replication jitter alone.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting modes

- **Empty overlay_fault rows despite verified overlay outages** — widen coalesce lists after fieldsummary exposes renamed telemetry keys; confirm earliest bounds cover incident windows and indexes listed in spl macros remain searchable for service accounts executing searches.

- **peer_oper_latest frozen while CLI churns** — duplicate leaf_hostname tokens across redundant syslog relays collapse distinct switches—enforce unique sourcetype transforms injecting mgmt_ip into host fields before stats grouping.

- **rt_bucket perpetually RT_UNSPECIFIED** — collectors omit textual route_family fields—capture raw syslog snippets temporarily without widening regex prohibitively across noisy EPS lanes.

- **underlay_mtu_below_vxlan_budget noise inside labs** — clone searches with relaxed thresholds tagged environment=lab so sandbox fabrics running nested overlays do not page production transports.

- **esi_df_instability during NIC teaming edits** — dual-homed hosts may reshuffle DF winners briefly—tie alarms to persistence timers exceeding graceful convergence budgets documented per vendor release notes.

- **bum_replication_anomaly spikes yet multicast routing stable** — ingress replication counters oscillate whenever Type-3 routes withdraw during RR churn though eventual flooding paths recover—pair Splunk timelines with **show vxlan flood** or vendor equivalents before blaming multicast cores alone.

- **evpn_control_plane_flap overlapping BGP graceful-restart drills** — correlate Splunk last_seen deltas against BGP neighbor uptime CLI outputs so intentional stale-route periods do not masquerade as hard failures.

- **vn_segment_count explosions while idle** — malformed vni strings slip through tonumber filters—inspect collector JSON for truncated hexadecimal representations causing bogus distinct counts.

- **Type-5 oscillations spanning DCI** — WAN QoS reorder may flap EVPN IP-prefix advertisements—engage transport SMEs before altering BGP policies tied to stretched subnets.

- **Duplicate syslog plus telemetry rows** — streaming bursts occasionally duplicate alerts already asserted via syslog within seconds—dedupe host overlay_fault _time windows before paging bridges reopen overnight.

- **SC4S duplicate injection** — syslog relays cloning TCP feeds twice inflate hits counters—audit RSyslog-forwarding diagrams referencing Splunk Connect for Syslog guidelines before rewriting SPL thresholds upward blindly.

- **fabric_site lookup stale entries** — suppression misses rename events inside automation catalogs—refresh CSV governance weekly alongside CMDB reconciliation rituals finance auditors already demand.

## SPL

```spl
(index=network_ops OR index=dc_fabric OR index=sdn_ops OR index=os OR index=cisco_dc)
(sourcetype="vxlan:vtep:reachability" OR sourcetype="vxlan:evpn:routes" OR sourcetype="vxlan:underlay:mtu" OR sourcetype="vxlan:bum:replication" OR sourcetype="cisco:nxos:syslog" OR sourcetype="arista:eos:syslog" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:syslog" OR sourcetype="juniper:junos:jti:openconfig")
earliest=-4h@m
| eval route_family=upper(trim(coalesce(evpn_route_type, nlri_rt, bgp_evpn_rt, route_type, "")))
| eval rt_bucket=case(
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?2|MAC.?IP"), "RT2_MACIP_TYPE2",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?3|IMET|INCLUSIVE"), "RT3_IMET_TYPE3",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?5|PREFIX"), "RT5_IP_PREFIX_TYPE5",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?4|ESI|DF"), "RT4_ESI_DF_TYPE4",
    match(route_family,"(?i)TYPE.?1|AUTO.?DISCOVERY"), "RT1_AD_TYPE1",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)mac.?mobility"), "RT2_MACIP_TYPE2",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)type.?5"), "RT5_IP_PREFIX_TYPE5",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)inclusive|imet"), "RT3_IMET_TYPE3",
    "RT_UNSPECIFIED")
| eval vxlan_udp_port=tonumber(coalesce(dst_udp_port, udp_dport, udp_dest_port, vxlan_udp_port, 4789))
| eval outer_mtu=tonumber(coalesce(underlay_mtu, pmtu, path_mtu, ""))
| eval vni_key=tonumber(coalesce(vni, vxlan_vni, evi_id, l2_vni, ""))
| eval dst_vtep=trim(coalesce(vtep_peer_ip, peer_vtep, nve_peer, dst_vtep_ip, ""))
| eval peer_state=lower(trim(coalesce(nve_peer_state, peer_oper_state, vtep_peer_state, reach_state, "")))
| eval overlay_fault=case(
    peer_state IN ("down","gone","inactive","failed","missing") OR match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(nve.*down|peer.*lost|vtep.*down|tunnel.*down)"), "loss_vtep_reachability",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(bgp.*reset|evpn.*flap|notification.*sent|afi.?25.*sa.?70|l2vpn.?evpn)"), "evpn_control_plane_flap",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(mac.?move|mobility|duplicate.?mac|sticky)"), "type2_mac_mobility_storm",
    isnotnull(outer_mtu) AND outer_mtu>0 AND outer_mtu<1554, "underlay_mtu_below_vxlan_budget",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(esi.*duplicate|df.*election|ether.?segment|single.?active|all.?active)"), "esi_df_instability",
    match(lower(_raw),"(?i)(bum|ingress.?repl|vxlan.?flood|unknown.?unicast)"), "bum_replication_anomaly",
    null())
| where isnotnull(overlay_fault)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen,
    latest(peer_state) as peer_oper_latest,
    latest(rt_bucket) as evpn_route_class,
    latest(vxlan_udp_port) as udp_port_seen,
    latest(outer_mtu) as mtu_observed,
    dc(vni_key) as vn_segment_count,
    values(dst_vtep) as peer_list,
    count as hits
  by overlay_fault, leaf_hostname, fabric_site, host
| eval last_seen_ts=strftime(last_seen,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %Z")
| sort -last_seen
| head 350
| table overlay_fault leaf_hostname fabric_site peer_oper_latest evpn_route_class udp_port_seen mtu_observed vn_segment_count peer_list hits last_seen_ts last_seen
```

## Visualization

(1) **Single-value** tiles per **`fabric_site`** summarizing **`overlay_fault`** severity mix. (2) **Timechart** **`hits`** by **`overlay_fault`** with **`fabric_site`** overlays. (3) **Heatmap** **`evpn_route_class`** × **`leaf_hostname`**. (4) **Table** worst **`overlay_fault`** rows with **`peer_list`**, **`mtu_observed`**, **`udp_port_seen`**. (5) **Sparkline** **`type2_mac_mobility_storm`** density vs **`vn_segment_count`** for mobility correlation. (6) **Choropleth / site map** optional when **`fabric_site`** encodes geography metadata.

## Known False Positives

**Live mobility bursts** — **Type 2 MAC/IP** advertisement surges occur during **VMware vMotion**, **Hyper-V Live Migration**, or storage live-migrations while **BGP** peers remain **Established**—suppress **`type2_mac_mobility_storm`** buckets using **`CHG`** identifiers referencing **`DRS`** maintenance modes plus **`hypervisor_cluster`** CMDB **`maintenance=true`** windows opened ahead of migrations documented inside virtualization runbooks.

**Planned hypervisor drains** — **ESXi** evacuation oscillates **`Anycast`** gateway **`VTEP`** sources behind shared first-hop relays so **`loss_vtep_reachability`** rows spike transiently though bridging stays intact until evacuation completes—cross-check **`fabric_site`** suppress macros tied to compute **`CHG`** tickets before escalating **`transport`** optics incidents alone.

**BGP graceful-restart on route-reflectors** — **`evpn_control_plane_flap`** fingerprints resemble outages during **`BGP`** **`RFC 4724`** graceful-restart timers while **`EVPN`** routes remain **`STALE`** intentionally—extend suppress lists using **`graceful_restart_active`** telemetry peers expose plus **`compare`** **`BGP`** **`neighbor`** **`restart`** **`timer`** **`CLI`** values versus **`Splunk`** **`last_seen`** deltas beneath configured **`stale-route`** intervals archived inside **`BGP`** **`policy`** references **`transport`** SMEs publish quarterly.

**Anycast-RP / multicast-core choreography** — **Type 3 inclusive multicast** routing churn spikes during **`PIM-SM`** **`Anycast-RP`** standby swaps—**`ingress`** **`replication`** counters fluctuate until multicast **`RP`** convergence finishes—suppress using **`multicast`** maintenance **`CRQ`** identifiers tied to **`spine`** **`Rendezvous-Point`** work rather than instantaneous overlay floods alone.

**PMTU ghosts above synthetic baselines** — **`underlay_mtu_below_vxlan_budget`** may surface only on selective elephant flows exceeding MSS samples your smaller **`ping`** tests used—layer **`interface`** **`fragment`** **`drop`** counters and targeted jumbo probes between **`VTEP`** pairs when Splunk **`mtu_observed`** oscillates near thresholds.

**Fabric automation duplicate-gateway warnings** — **NDFC** or **Apstra** rebuilds temporarily raise **Anycast** IP duplicate-detection syslog while **`bridge-domain`** templates re-stage—suppress when **`automation_pipeline_id`** tokens map to known fabric provisioning **`CHG`** tickets **`NetOps`** automation teams already track in ticketing bridges.

**Software VTEP cross-talk** — **Linux** defaults sometimes use **`UDP`** **8472** while hardware fabrics standardize on **`4789`**—segregate lab indexes or enforce Splunk **`where`** expectations on **`udp_port_seen`** so development clusters do not pollute production **`overlay_fault`** dashboards curated for **`IANA`** port discipline.

## References

- [IETF RFC 7348 — Virtual eXtensible Local Area Network (VXLAN)](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7348)
- [IETF RFC 7432 — BGP MPLS-Based Ethernet VPN (EVPN)](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc7432)
- [IETF RFC 8365 — A Network Virtualization Overlay Solution Using EVPN](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8365)
- [Cisco Nexus 9000 Series NX-OS VXLAN Configuration Guide](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/dcn/nx-os/nexus9000/103x/configuration/vxlan/cisco-nexus-9000-series-nx-os-vxlan-configuration-guide-release-103x.html)
- [Arista EOS VXLAN/EVPN Technical Information (TOI)](https://www.arista.com/en/support/toi/eos-4-30-2f)
- [Juniper Apstra Documentation](https://www.juniper.net/documentation/product/us/en/apstra/)
- [NVIDIA Cumulus Linux — Ethernet Virtual Private Network (EVPN)](https://docs.nvidia.com/networking-ethernet-software/cumulus-linux-58/Layer-2/Ethernet-Virtual-Private-Network-EVPN/)
- [Splunk Add-on for Cisco Nexus (Splunkbase 6876)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6876)
- [Splunk Add-on for Arista (Splunkbase 6797)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6797)
- [Splunk Add-on for Juniper (Splunkbase 2847)](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2847)
